name: Walking on the water
notes: After the feeding of the five thousand, Matthew, Mark, and John all recount Jesus walking on the sea to the disciples in the storm-tossed ship. The shared core runs from his sending the disciples ahead and withdrawing to a mountain, through the contrary wind in the midst of the sea, to his coming to them walking on the water, the word 'It is I; be not afraid,' and his entering the ship. Each evangelist keeps his own vantage: Matthew and Mark detail the constraining of the disciples and the multitude sent away, John alone notes the crowd's purpose to make him a king and the measured five and twenty or thirty furlongs they had rowed. Matthew alone tells of Peter walking on the water, his cry 'Lord, save me,' and the worship 'Thou art the Son of God,' which stand without parallel and are left to read in place. Mark closes with the disciples' amazement and the hardness of heart concerning the loaves, a complementary closing vantage on the same night.
